hero.badge: Industrial OS · ERP · MES · Office · CAD · AI — on a single platform
hero.subtitle: ERP, MES, quality, inventory, CAD and AI in one place. From design to shipment, on a shared database — no silos or brittle integrations, with the entire operation on one screen.
hero.demoNote: Read-only demo · 30-min session · no card required

flow.title: From design to shipment, one single system.
flow.subtitle: Every stage of the operation lives on the same platform — no app-hopping, no losing the thread of information.

galaxy.title: One OS, every program.
galaxy.subtitle: Each department is a native program of the same system — not loose apps glued together with integrations.

controlTower.name: Control Tower
controlTower.value: Live readiness, OEE, andons and holds.

mes.name: MES · Floor
mes.value: Poka-yoke, backflush and andon per station.

ai.name: AI · CIDE
ai.value: Your data analyst with built-in AI.

platform.title: One platform. The whole operation.
platform.subtitle: Click any module to see the detail.

controlTower.title: Control tower
controlTower.desc: Operation visibility with live KPIs: readiness, OEE, throughput and alerts.
controlTower.details: Mission Control and the line tower consolidate readiness, plan vs actual, andons, holds and line status in a single panel. For directors and supervisors.

inventory.title: Inventory and kitting
inventory.desc: Inventory, kitting and e-kanban replenishment to the line, with shortage alerts.
inventory.details: Per-station kitting from the routing, pull replenishment (e-kanban) and cycle counts. Connected to floor execution.

mes.title: Floor execution (MES)
mes.desc: MES operator: poka-yoke, material backflush and andon per station.
mes.details: The operator scans, the system validates the part number (poka-yoke), deducts material (backflush) and records progress. Holds for quality, skill and shortage.

quality.title: Quality and MRB
quality.desc: Holds that quarantine the lot and block consumption, with an MRB flow.
quality.details: Reject capture → quarantine → MRB review → disposition (use-as-is/rework/scrap/RTV) with signature and where-used. Blocks consumption of the held Work Order.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.desc: NestJS + Next.js, TypeORM and multi-tenant design. Modular monolith.
architecture.details: Modular NestJS backend, Next.js frontend, TypeORM and an architecture ready for multi-tenant (per-organization isolation).

roles.title: Roles by position
roles.desc: Per-organization isolation with roles and permissions by floor position.
roles.details: Plant roles (operator, material handler, supervisor, planning, quality/MRB, industrial engineering) with per-action permissions and per-plant/line scope.

why.title: It's not another suite. It's an operating system.

db.title: A single database
db.body: From design to shipment with no fragile integrations or silos between departments.

planToFloor.title: From plan to floor, live
planToFloor.body: Publish the plan and execute it with MES, backflush and quality holds in real time.

traceability.title: Native traceability
traceability.body: Serial/lot, as-built and where-used ready for customer audits and containment.

ai.title: AI with your plant's context
ai.body: CIDE understands all your modules and data — not a generic chatbot bolted on top.

solutions.title: Built for contract manufacturing.
solutions.subtitle: Expand each capability to see what it actually does.

items.title: Traceability and genealogy
items.summary: Serial/lot per unit, as-built and where-used ready for audit.
points: Cradle-to-grave genealogy: lot/reel by part number, station, operator and time.
Reverse where-used for containment when a lot turns out questionable.
Certificate of Conformance (CoC) per order or per shipment.

items.title: From plan to floor
items.summary: Publish the Work Order, kit material to the station and run it with live backflush.
points: Poka-yoke by part number and skill/quality validations per station.
Automatic material backflush when progress is confirmed.
Andon and holds that block consumption of the held lot.

items.title: BOM and engineering
items.summary: Routing per model, materials per station with usage factor and revisions.
points: Product structure and routing per model, versioned.
Materials per station with usage factor for backflush.
2D ⇄ 3D line layout to design the flow before executing it.

enterprise.title: Security and control.
enterprise.body: Console access requires administrator approval. Created accounts stay queued until validated. The session is signed (HMAC-SHA256) and stored in an HttpOnly cookie.

items.title: Account approval
items.body: New sign-ups notify the admin and stay pending.

items.title: Granular roles
items.body: Admin, Engineering, Production, Quality, Inventory, Finance.

items.title: Signed sessions
items.body: HttpOnly cookies + HMAC signature with configurable expiry.

items.q: Does AXOS OS replace an ERP or an MES?
items.a: The product's ambition is to operate as both on one industrial platform: ERP for materials, costs and supply chain; MES for floor, execution, quality and traceability.

items.q: Does the demo use real customer data?
items.a: No. The public demo should be treated as a sample, read-only environment; no customers, certifications or invented commercial metrics are shown.
Public login exists, but new accounts are subject to administrative approval and roles before entering the console.

items.q: Does AXOS rely on external integrations to tell its story?
items.a: The landing describes native capabilities of the repository: ERP, MES, Office, CAD, quality, analytics and AI modules already exposed on internal routes.

finalCta.title: Enter the OS. Evaluate the full flow.
finalCta.body: Sign in if you already have access, request an account for approval, or open the read-only demo to walk through the experience without inflated promises.

footer.tagline: The industrial operating system for modern electronics manufacturing.

toast.demoError: Could not start the demo. Please try again.

bento.title: We don't tell you. We show you.
bento.subtitle: Every piece of the operation, live and connected to the same database. This is what happens inside the OS.

planToFloor.title: Publish the order and watch it run.
planToFloor.body: MES with poka-yoke, andon per station and material backflush. The plan stops being a PDF: it's the floor, in real time.

oee.title: Live KPIs
oee.body: Readiness, OEE and andons on one screen.

trace.title: Every unit tells its story: serial, lot and as-built.

quality.body: A hold blocks consumption of the lot until MRB disposition.

cide.question: Why did FPY drop on SMT-2 yesterday?
cide.answerPost: (solder skips). The hold is already active; check the reflow profile.

office.body: SOPs and reports connected to the real work, inside the OS.
